title: Small-cap stocks bought by FIIs in the March quarter
summary: Amid all the selling by FIIs in the quarter gone by, these select smallcaps bucked the trend and received buying interest.

title: RBI proposals for large NBFCs leaves Tata Sons as only unlisted one
summary: The RBI has proposed changes to regulations for non-banking financial companies, simplifying criteria for upper layer NBFCs. The status of Tata Sons, which faces internal disagreements among its shareholders on a listing, remains unclear as it had previously surrendered its NBFC licence.

title: Meet broad-based rules while serving AIFs, Sebi clarifies
summary: In a push to tighten regulations, Sebi has laid down fresh guidelines for asset management companies overseeing alternative investment funds. Each scheme is now required to include at least 20 investors, ensuring that no individual investor exceeds a 25% stake in the fund's total corpus.

title: D-Street goes green after streak of red
summary: After enduring a challenging six-week slump, Indian stock markets made a notable comeback, with the Nifty and Sensex soaring significantly. The positive momentum was fueled by heightened investor enthusiasm surrounding US-Iran ceasefire discussions. This surge not only elevated the market capitalizations of various companies but also attracted foreign portfolio investors as they shifted to net buyers.

title: Global Funds Flee Indian Stocks at Record Pace on Growth Fears
summary: Global funds are dumping Indian equities at a record clip as an energy shock from the US-Iran war threatens to derail the outlook of the world’s fastest-growing major economy.

title: Why is Ola Electric share price skyrocketing in FY27? Explained
summary: Ola Electric share price has skyrocketed from  ₹23.92 apiece to  ₹40.40 per share on the NSE, logging around 70% rise in one month

title: Kalshi wins reprieve from Arizona criminal case at CFTC’s urging
summary: Kalshi Inc. won a temporary reprieve from criminal prosecution in Arizona when a judge suspended the case at the behest of a federal regulator.

title: Sam Altman's home hit by Molotov cocktail, OpenAI CEO shares rare family photo in first reaction — ‘We try to be…’
summary: San Francisco police arrested a 20-year-old man for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's home in the city and for making threats outside the artificial intelligence startup's headquarters, OpenAI said on Friday.

title: Concurrent Gainers: 11 stocks that gained for five straight days, rallying 15–30%
FIIs sell Indian equities worth Rs 48,213 crore in April, so far; FY26 sell-off balloons to Rs 1.79 lakh crore
summary: Foreign investors continued their significant sell-off in Indian equities, offloading Rs 48,213 crore in April alone. Despite this trend, domestic markets ended Friday with strong gains, buoyed by buying from local institutional investors. Market direction hinges on upcoming US-Iran peace talks, which could impact crude prices and investor sentiment.

title: Global funds flee Indian stocks at record pace on growth fears
summary: Global funds are exiting Indian equities at a record pace, driven by an energy shock from the US-Iran war and a lack of compelling AI-linked investment narratives. This sustained selling has pressured local shares, with over $600 billion wiped off their value from last year's peak, despite strong domestic inflows.

title: Regulator bars 39 entities for alleged manipulation of RRP Semicon share price
summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India has intervened in the case of RRP Semiconductor, taking serious measures against 39 entities implicated in suspected stock price manipulation. The regulator discovered a systematic approach that inflated the company's share price beyond sustainable limits.

title: Guardrails for MFIs need to continue
summary: In a recent advisory, Sa-Dhan, a key player in the microfinance arena, underscores the necessity of upholding lending standards for financially vulnerable populations. These standards not only mitigate the risk of over-borrowing but also enhance trust within the financial ecosystem. The microfinance sector continues to demonstrate its resilience amid market fluctuations.

title: War? Inflation? The market is moving on.
summary: The cease-fire announced on Tuesday led to the best day for the stock market in almost exactly a year. But it’s too soon to look past the war.

title: IBM to pay $17 million as settlement linked to anti-DEI probe — What was the tech major accused of?
summary: The settlement comes as the first resolution from the US Justice Department's unit formed last year called “Civil Rights Fraud Initiative” to crack down on DEI policies using a civil anti-fraud law.
